title: The Birth of the Transistor: William Shockley's Groundbreaking Patent of 1948

scenes.text: June 26 in the History of Computer Science marks the birth of the modern transistor.
On this day in 1948, William Shockley filed a patent for the junction transistor—a design that paved the way for practical, mass-produced semiconductors.
This innovation replaced bulky vacuum tubes, making computers smaller and more reliable.
Shockley’s invention set the stage for integrated circuits and the microchips that now power our world.
